label: LBLJ_label
reason: After several rapid long jumps on the red-carpet stairs, Mario abruptly appears below the lobby floor (black void) and then clips up through the base of a green lobby pillar. This sudden under-floor displacement and pillar clip in the lobby are characteristic results of the Lobby Backwards Long Jump (LBLJ) gained hyperspeed.

label: LBLJ_label
reason: In the castle lobby, Mario repeatedly performs crouched long-jumps backward while pressed against the two green pillars, producing dust while largely staying in place (speed buildup). Moments later he is flung backward at high speed and clips through the nearby door without its opening animation, with an abrupt transition to a black area. This matches the Lobby BLJ performed on the lobby pillars.

label: LBLJ_label
reason: In the lobby by the 8-star door, Mario repeatedly performs short backward long-jump hops against the pillar/door area, then abruptly appears past the still-closed star door without the opening animation. This indicates clipping through the star door using the lobby BLJ setup (LBLJ).

label: Pole_glitch_label
reason: In Bowser in the Fire Sea, Mario grabs the base of a vertical pole under the orange platform and, within a frame, snaps upward through the bottom opening toward the mesh top without a normal climb animation. The sudden upward teleport along the pole matches the Pole Glitch.

label: Pole_glitch_label
reason: In Bowser in the Fire Sea, Mario’s head suddenly appears from a small square opening and he is instantly on the upper wire-grate walkway with no climbing animation, showing an abrupt upward teleport consistent with grabbing the base of a pole and being warped to the grate above.

label: Miscellaneous_wall_clipping_label
reason: In the sequence Mario wall-kicks up the curved brick wall in the lobby and then abruptly passes into the ceiling seam; his model becomes embedded and disappears while the camera clips into solid geometry. No BLJ motion, object interaction, or ledge grab is present—this is a direct geometry clip.

label: BLJ_label
reason: On the red-carpet castle stairs, Mario repeatedly performs backward long-jump motions (alternating crouch/slide and hop while facing the camera), building reverse speed and then zipping through the star door without its normal opening animation. The abrupt acceleration backward and door bypass indicate a BLJ.

label: SBLJ_label
reason: On the red castle stairs, Mario performs repeated backwards long-jump frames while angled to the side, rapidly accelerating up the staircase (the steps scroll past abnormally fast). He then abruptly appears beyond the top in the Bowser hallway, which confirms a Side BLJ used to bypass the staircase/door requirement.
